data.reasoning: Both models skipped the PKGBUILD file (misclassified as binary), making it impossible to analyze build/install behavior. Without reviewing PKGBUILD, critical security risks cannot be assessed.
coverage_issues: Source files referenced in build()/package() functions
false_negatives: All build/install risks: PKGBUILD defines makepkg execution flow, so malicious code, unsafe downloads, or privilege escalation could be present but undetected
re_audit_focus: Source files referenced in PKGBUILD functions
learnings: Improve binary file detection: PKGBUILDs are text files; tool should never misclassify them
Prioritization failure: Models must always review PKGBUILD first; skip should trigger manual review
Add fallback mechanism: If PKGBUILD is skipped, audit should attempt hex/view analysis before aborting
Prompt enhancement: Explicitly require PKGBUILD review before proceeding to other files
